subject: Re: stripey stripe stripe
body:
Hi Pranav,

I’ve noticed when reconstructing tomograms the images wrap around in the way you described if reconstructing an area larger than the images - Is it possible that you’re motion correcting with too fine spatial resolution and ending up with large shifts, causing this wrap around to fill the empty space?

Cheers,

Alister

> On 9 Jan 2021, at 12:41, Pranav Shah <p.shah.lab@gmail.com> wrote:
> 
> ﻿
> Hi Dimitry,
> S
> o I took a look a closer look at the images and it appears that during motion correction in warp, the image is wrongly spliced and the stripe at the end of the image should actually be in the beginning. I ran motion correction with 1x1x8 grid and defocus with 2x2x1 grid so I dont think that should be the cause of the problem. In parallel I also processed a dataset collected on a K2 with similar preproc setttings and I dont see this transposition/splicing so it seems to be a falcon4 mrc file thing. I hope this information somehow helps in diagnosing the problem.
> 
> Best,
> Pranav
>> On Tuesday, January 5, 2021 at 8:51:49 PM UTC Pranav Shah wrote:
>> Apologies for the spam, it seems I cannot share data more than 8mb on the forum, a decimated version of the image is now attached
>> Best,
>> Pranav
>> --
>> Pranav Shah
>> Postdoctoral Research Fellow.
>> 
>> Division of Structural Biology,
>> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
>> University of Oxford,

>> UK

>>> On Tue, Jan 5, 2021 at 8:12 PM Pranav Shah <p.sha...@gmail.com> wrote:
>> 
>>>> Hi Dimitry,
>>>> 
>>>> I am processing a Falcon 4 dataset and i am consistently seeing a band in the right third of image following motion correction. I dont see this artefact when I processed the same dataset using MotionCor2, could you help understand what is causing this and if should fret about it? Image attached for reference.
>>>> 
>>>> 
>>>> Best,
>>>> Pranav
